Term.definition: Errors that occur while the program is running, often causing it to crash or behave unexpectedly.
The meaning or interpretation of code, affecting how programs behave or produce results.
The entry point for Java applications; where program execution begins.
The set of rules that defines the structure of valid Java code.
A data type representing two values: true or false, often used for logical conditions.
A number type in Java that can represent fractions and decimals, such as double or float.
Division between two integers, discarding any fractional part, returning only the whole number.
A sequence of if-else statements used to check multiple conditions in order.
An if-else statement placed inside another if or else block.
A symbol that tells the compiler to perform specific mathematical or logical operations.
A control structure that executes code only if a specified condition is true.
A value passed to a method or function when it is called.
The set of rules that defines how Java code must be written.
A text-based interface for displaying program output and receiving user input.
The position indicator showing where the next character will appear or be acted upon.
A combination of variables, operators, and values that produces a single result value.
A data type in Java used to store a single 16-bit Unicode character.
A single instruction in code that performs an action, like assignment or output.
A value or variable on which an operator performs an action in an expression.
A single cycle through the body of a loop in programming.
A single instruction in a programming language executed by the computer.
Removing the fractional part of a number, leaving only the integer portion.
Automatic conversion of one data type to another by the compiler.
Division operation where the fractional part is discarded, returning only the integer quotient.
A data type representing true or false values, used for logical operations in programming.
A program that translates source code into machine code or bytecode for execution.
A loop that repeats code while a condition remains true.
A symbol that performs mathematical operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.
An operator (=) that assigns the value on the right to the variable on the left.
A text-based interface for displaying program output and accepting user input.
The position indicator showing where the next character will appear on the screen.
A complete instruction in a programming language that performs an action.
To repeat a process or set of instructions, usually within a loop structure.
A control flow statement for repeated execution of a block of code with a counter variable.
A text-based interface for displaying output or receiving input from a program.
The rules that determine the order in which operations are evaluated in an expression.
In switch statements, execution continues into subsequent cases if break is omitted.
An expression that evaluates to either true or false in Java.
To assign an initial value to a variable at the time of its declaration.
To define a variable's name and type, reserving space in memory for it.
An expression that evaluates to either true or false.
A loop that never terminates because its exit condition is never met.
A Java method used to print text to the console, followed by a new line.
A control structure that repeats a block of code a specific number of times.
A low-level programming language closely related to machine code, using symbolic instructions.
The set of rules that defines the structure of statements in a programming language.
Programs that translate code from high-level languages to machine language for execution.
A number is divisible by another if it divides evenly with no remainder.
An expression that evaluates to true or false, used to control program flow.
